The reheat Rankine cycle has the following states:
- State 1: saturated liquid at \(P_1 = 10\) kPa.
- Pump process to state 2 at \(P_2 = 8.7\) MPa.
- State 3: superheated vapor at \(P_3 = 8.7\) MPa, \(T_3 = 533^\circ\)C.
- HPT process to state 4 at \(P_4 = 2.3\) MPa.
- Reheat to state 5 at \(P_5 = 2.3\) MPa, \(T_5 = 544^\circ\)C.
- LPT process to state 6 at \(P_6 = 10\) kPa.

From steam tables at \(P_1 = 10\) kPa (saturated liquid): \(h_1 = 191.81\) kJ/kg, \(v_1 = 0.001010\) m³/kg, \(s_1 = 0.6492\) kJ/(kg·K), \(h_g = 2584.6\) kJ/kg, \(s_g = 8.1502\) kJ/(kg·K).

For the pump (incompressible liquid approximation):  
\(w_{p,s} = v_1(P_2 - P_1) = 0.001010 \times (8700 - 10) = 8.78\) kJ/kg.  
\(h_{2s} = h_1 + w_{p,s} = 200.59\) kJ/kg.  
Pump efficiency: \(\eta_p = w_{p,s}/w_p\), so \(w_p = w_{p,s}/\eta_p = 8.78/0.85 = 10.33\) kJ/kg.  
Thus, \(h_2 = h_1 + w_p = 202.14\) kJ/kg.

From steam tables (with interpolation for exact \(P = 8.7\) MPa): \(h_3 = 3481.7\) kJ/kg, \(s_3 = 6.795\) kJ/(kg·K).

For isentropic HPT: \(s_{4s} = s_3 = 6.795\) kJ/(kg·K) at \(P_4 = 2.3\) MPa (superheated; interpolated from tables at nearby pressures). This yields \(h_{4s} = 3052.8\) kJ/kg.  
HPT efficiency: \(\eta_{HPT} = (h_3 - h_4)/(h_3 - h_{4s})\).  
Thus, \(h_3 - h_4 = 0.89 \times (3481.7 - 3052.8) = 381.8\) kJ/kg, so \(h_4 = 3100\) kJ/kg (rounded).

From steam tables (with interpolation for exact \(P = 2.3\) MPa): \(h_5 = 3567.7\) kJ/kg, \(s_5 = 7.524\) kJ/(kg·K).

For isentropic LPT: \(s_{6s} = s_5 = 7.524\) kJ/(kg·K) at \(P_6 = 10\) kPa (wet vapor).  
Quality: \(x_{6s} = (7.524 - 0.6492)/7.501 \approx 0.9166\).  
\(h_{6s} = 191.81 + 0.9166 \times 2392.8 \approx 2385.1\) kJ/kg.  
LPT efficiency: \(\eta_{LPT} = (h_5 - h_6)/(h_5 - h_{6s})\).  
Thus, \(h_5 - h_6 = 0.82 \times (3567.7 - 2385.1) = 969.7\) kJ/kg, so \(h_6 = 2598\) kJ/kg (rounded).

Pump work: \(w_\text{pump} = h_2 - h_1 = 10.33\) kJ/kg.  
Heat input: \(q_\text{in} = (h_3 - h_2) + (h_5 - h_4) = 3747.2\) kJ/kg.  
HPT work: \(w_\text{HPT} = h_3 - h_4 = 381.8\) kJ/kg.  
LPT work: \(w_\text{LPT} = h_5 - h_6 = 969.7\) kJ/kg.  
Net work: \(w_\text{net} = w_\text{HPT} + w_\text{LPT} - w_\text{pump} = 1341.2\) kJ/kg.  
Thermal efficiency: \(\eta_\text{th} = w_\text{net}/q_\text{in} = 0.358\).  
Net power output: \(\dot{W}_\text{net} = \dot{m} \times w_\text{net} = 125930\) kW.

**Reasoning**

The reheat Rankine cycle states are defined as follows: state 1 is saturated liquid at \(P_1 = 47\) kPa; the pump raises the pressure to 15 MPa (state 2); the boiler delivers the fluid at 15 MPa, 430 °C (state 3); the high-pressure turbine expands to 2.3 MPa (state 4); the reheater raises the temperature to 443 °C at 2.3 MPa (state 5); the low-pressure turbine expands to the condenser pressure (state 6).

Steam-table values (IAPWS-based) supply \(h\) and \(s\) at each state. For the pump the isentropic work is \(w_{ps} = v_1(P_2 - P_1)\); the actual work follows from the given isentropic efficiency \(\eta_p = 0.88\), yielding \(h_2 = h_1 + w_{ps}/\eta_p\). Isentropic turbine exits (\(h_{4s}\), \(h_{6s}\)) are found by equating entropy to the inlet value and locating the state at the respective exit pressure (wet-region quality calculation when necessary). Actual turbine enthalpies are obtained from the definitions \(\eta_{HPT} = (h_3 - h_4)/(h_3 - h_{4s})\) and \(\eta_{LPT} = (h_5 - h_6)/(h_5 - h_{6s})\).

Component specific works are \(w_{pump} = h_2 - h_1\), \(w_{HPT} = h_3 - h_4\), \(w_{LPT} = h_5 - h_6\), and \(w_{net} = w_{HPT} + w_{LPT} - w_{pump}\). Heat transfers are \(q_{in} = (h_3 - h_2) + (h_5 - h_4)\) and \(q_{out} = h_6 - h_1\). Thermal efficiency is \(\eta_{th} = w_{net}/q_{in}\). Net power follows from \(\dot{W}_{net} = \dot{m}w_{net}\).

Entropy generation per unit mass for each component follows from the entropy balance:
- Adiabatic pump, HPT, LPT: \(s_{gen} = s_{out} - s_{in}\)
- Boiler and reheater (heat from reservoir at \(T_H = 881.1\) K): \(s_{gen} = (s_{out} - s_{in}) - q/T_H\)
- Condenser (heat to reservoir at \(T_C = 340.3\) K): \(s_{gen} = (s_1 - s_6) + q_{out}/T_C\)

Total entropy generation is the sum of the six component values. All calculations close the energy balance to within round-off error (energy-balance error = 0).

- HPT expands to state 4 at \(P_4 = 2.6\) MPa.
- State 5: superheated vapor at \(P_5 = 2.6\) MPa, \(T_5 = 423^\circ\)C (after reheater).
- LPT expands to state 6 at \(P_6 = 30\) kPa.

From steam tables:
- At 30 kPa (saturated): \(h_1 = h_f = 289.23\) kJ/kg, \(s_1 = s_f = 0.9439\) kJ/(kg·K), \(v_1 = v_f = 0.001022\) m³/kg, \(h_g = 2625.3\) kJ/kg, \(s_g = 7.7686\) kJ/(kg·K), \(h_{fg} = 2336.07\) kJ/kg, \(s_{fg} = 6.8247\) kJ/(kg·K).

Isentropic pump work: \(w_{ps} = v_1(P_2 - P_1) = 0.001022 \times (6600 - 30) = 6.714\) kJ/kg.  
Thus, \(h_{2s} = h_1 + w_{ps} = 295.94\) kJ/kg.  
With \(\eta_{pump} = 0.89\), actual pump work \(w_{pump} = w_{ps}/\eta_{pump} = 7.54\) kJ/kg.  
Thus, \(h_2 = h_1 + w_{pump} = 296.77\) kJ/kg.  
For compressed liquid at state 2 (\(h_2\), \(P_2\)), \(T_2 \approx 69.3^\circ\)C and \(s_2 = 0.945\) kJ/(kg·K) (from \(s_f(T_2)\) corrected by \(-\beta v \Delta P\)).

From superheated steam tables (with interpolation for exact \(P\) and \(T\)):  
\(h_3 = 3172.3\) kJ/kg, \(s_3 = 6.521\) kJ/(kg·K).  
\(h_5 = 3289.5\) kJ/kg, \(s_5 = 7.077\) kJ/(kg·K).

For HPT (\(\eta_{HPT} = 0.82\)): \(s_{4s} = s_3 = 6.521\) kJ/(kg·K).  
At \(P_4 = 2.6\) MPa (interpolated tables at 250°C and 300°C): \(h_{4s} = 2947.8\) kJ/kg.  
\(\eta_{HPT} = (h_3 - h_4)/(h_3 - h_{4s})\) gives \(h_4 = 2988.2\) kJ/kg.  
At state 4 (\(h_4\), \(P_4\)): \(s_4 = 6.596\) kJ/(kg·K).

For LPT (\(\eta_{LPT} = 0.83\)): \(s_{6s} = s_5 = 7.077\) kJ/(kg·K).  
Quality \(x_{6s} = (s_{6s} - s_1)/s_{fg} = 0.8987\), so \(h_{6s} = 2388.93\) kJ/kg.  
\(\eta_{LPT} = (h_5 - h_6)/(h_5 - h_{6s})\) gives \(h_6 = 2542.0\) kJ/kg.  
At state 6: \(x_6 = 0.9644\), \(s_6 = 7.527\) kJ/(kg·K).

\(w_{LPT} = h_5 - h_6 = 747.5\) kJ/kg.  
\(w_{net} = w_{HPT} + w_{LPT} - w_{pump} = 924.0\) kJ/kg.  
\(q_{boiler} = h_3 - h_2 = 2875.53\) kJ/kg.  
\(q_{reheat} = h_5 - h_4 = 301.3\) kJ/kg.  
\(q_{in} = q_{boiler} + q_{reheat} = 3176.8\) kJ/kg.  
\(\eta_{th} = w_{net}/q_{in} = 0.2909\).  
\(\dot{W}_{net} = \dot{m} \times w_{net} = 53223\) kW.  
\(q_{out} = h_6 - h_1 = 2252.8\) kJ/kg (verifies energy balance: \(q_{in} - q_{out} = w_{net}\)).

Entropy generations (per unit mass, using \(T_H = 786.4\) K and \(T_C = 331.1\) K):  
Pump (adiabatic): \(s_{gen,pump} = s_2 - s_1 = 0.001\) kJ/(kg·K).  
Boiler: \(s_{gen,boiler} = (s_3 - s_2) - q_{boiler}/T_H = 1.900\) kJ/(kg·K).  
HPT (adiabatic): \(s_{gen,HPT} = s_4 - s_3 = 0.075\) kJ/(kg·K).  
Reheater: \(s_{gen,reheater} = (s_5 - s_4) - q_{reheat}/T_H = 0.098\) kJ/(kg·K).  
LPT (adiabatic): \(s_{gen,LPT} = s_6 - s_5 = 0.450\) kJ/(kg·K).  
Condenser: \(s_{gen,cond} = (s_1 - s_6) + q_{out}/T_C = 0.221\) kJ/(kg·K).  
Total: \(s_{gen,total} =\) sum of component values \(= 2.745\) kJ/(kg·K).  
Energy balance closes exactly (error = 0).

**Reasoning:**

State 1 is saturated liquid at 16 kPa. Properties (hf, sf, vf) were obtained by interpolation of saturated steam tables between 15 kPa and 20 kPa, with adjustment for exact saturation temperature at 16 kPa (T_sat ≈ 55.34 °C) using the Clapeyron relation for precision, yielding h₁, s₁, and v₁.

The pump is analyzed using the incompressible liquid approximation. Isentropic work is w_{p,s} = v₁(P₂ - P₁), giving h_{2s}. Actual pump work follows from the given isentropic efficiency η_pump = w_{p,s}/w_p, so h₂ = h₁ + w_pump. Entropy s₂ is found from s₂ ≈ s₁ + c_p ln(T₂/T₁) - v₁(P₂ - P₁)/T_avg (with c_p ≈ 4.184 kJ/kg·K and temperatures from enthalpy changes), resulting in s₂ < s₁.

State 3 (9.6 MPa, 420 °C) properties are obtained by interpolation in superheated steam tables at 10 MPa (h = 3151.86 kJ/kg, s = 6.2948 kJ/kg·K at 420 °C) followed by correction to 9.6 MPa. Corrections use (∂h/∂P)_T ≈ -19.5 kJ/kg per MPa and (∂s/∂P)_T ≈ -0.069 per MPa (from tabulated trends between 8 and 10 MPa), giving final h₃ and s₃.

For the HPT, isentropic expansion to 2.6 MPa sets s_{4s} = s₃. At 2.6 MPa, saturation properties are interpolated from 2.5 MPa tables; since s_{4s} > s_g, state 4s is superheated. T_{4s} is found iteratively from s(T) ≈ s_g + c_p ln(T/T_sat), then h_{4s} from h_g + c_p(T_{4s} - T_sat). Actual Δh follows from η_HPT = 0.89, giving h₄. Then s₄ is calculated as s_{4s} + c_p ln(T₄/T_{4s}) with T₄ from the extra enthalpy at constant pressure (c_p ≈ 2.3 kJ/kg·K).

State 5 (2.6 MPa, 406 °C) properties are interpolated from superheated tables at 2 MPa and 3 MPa at 400 °C, then adjusted by ≈6 °C using c_p ≈ 2.35 kJ/kg·K for both h and s increments, giving h₅ and s₅.

For the LPT, isentropic expansion sets s_{6s} = s₅. At 16 kPa, s_{6s} < s_g so quality x_{6s} = (s_{6s} - s_f)/s_{fg} and h_{6s} = h_f + x_{6s}h_{fg}. Actual Δh follows from η_LPT = 0.90, giving h₆. Actual quality x₆ = (h₆ - h_f)/h_{fg} then yields s₆ = s_f + x₆ s_{fg}.

Component specific works: w_pump as calculated above; w_HPT = h₃ - h₄; w_LPT = h₅ - h₆; w_net = (w_HPT + w_LPT) - w_pump. Heat input q_in = (h₃ - h₂) + (h₅ - h₄). Thermal efficiency η_th = w_net/q_in. Net power is ṁ × w_net.

Entropy generations per unit mass (all values in kJ/kg·K):
- Pump (adiabatic): s_gen,pump = s₂ - s₁.
- Boiler: s_gen,boiler = (s₃ - s₂) - q_boiler/T_source (T_source = 782 K).
- HPT (adiabatic): s_gen,HPT = s₄ - s₃.
- Reheater: s_gen,reheater = (s₅ - s₄) - q_reheat/T_source.
- LPT (adiabatic): s_gen,LPT = s₆ - s₅.
- Condenser: s_gen,cond = (s₁ - s₆) + q_out/T_sink (q_out = h₆ - h₁, T_sink = 315 K).
- Total: direct sum of all component s_gen values.

Energy balance error is computed as |q_in - q_out - w_net| (normalized per unit mass) and is zero within rounding. All table interpolations and corrections are consistent with IAPWS-based steam property trends; minor rounding applied to match typical reporting precision (1-2 decimals for h/w/q, 3-4 for s/s_gen).

Exergy destruction \(x_{dest} = T_0 s_{gen}\) per component. Second-law efficiency \(\eta_{II} = w_{net}/[q_{in}(1 - T_0/T_h)]\). All values cross-checked for consistency (energy balance closes to machine precision; exergy balance closes within rounding).

Superheated properties at state 3 (15 MPa, 569°C) were obtained by interpolation in steam tables between 500°C and 600°C values, using linear interpolation on h vs. T and ln(T) weighting on s for consistency with constant-c_p approximation. Isentropic expansion in HPT to P_reheat gave s_{4s} = s₃; state 4s properties at 1.8 MPa were found by interpolation in the superheated region (s_{4s} > s_g), yielding h_{4s}. Actual h₄ followed from η_HPT = (h₃ - h₄)/(h₃ - h_{4s}). State 5 properties (1.8 MPa, 416°C) were obtained by interpolation in superheated steam tables (using ideal-gas correction R ln(P_ref/P) on entropy and table trend on enthalpy). Isentropic expansion in LPT gave s_{6s} = s₅; quality x_{6s} = (s_{6s} - s_f)/s_{fg} at P_cond, then h_{6s} = h_f + x_{6s}h_{fg}. Actual h₆ followed from η_LPT = (h₅ - h₆)/(h₅ - h_{6s}); actual quality x₆ = (h₆ - h_f)/h_{fg} and s₆ = s_f + x₆ s_{fg}. All w and q values are direct differences of the state enthalpies. η_th = w_net/q_in and Ẇ_net = ṁ w_net. Each s_gen is computed from the entropy balance on the component: s_gen,pump = s₂ - s₁ (adiabatic), s_gen,HPT = s₄ - s₃ (adiabatic), s_gen,LPT = s₆ - s₅ (adiabatic), s_gen,boiler = (s₃ - s₂) - q_boiler/T_H, s_gen,reheater = (s₅ - s₄) - q_reheat/T_H, and s_gen,cond = (s₁ - s₆) + q_out/T_c (with q_out = h₆ - h₁). Total s_gen is the sum of component values. Flow exergies are ef_i = (h_i - h₀) - T₀(s_i - s₀) with h₀ = 104.89 kJ/kg and s₀ = 0.3674 kJ/(kg·K) at T₀, P₀. Exergy destructions follow as x_dest = T₀ s_gen (per unit mass) for each component; total is the sum. η_II = w_net/[q_in(1 - T₀/T_H)]. Energy balance closes exactly (q_in - q_out = w_net) so error = 0. All values are rounded consistently with steam-table precision.

- At \(48^\circ\)C (state 3, saturated liquid): \(h_3 = h_f = 267.29\) kJ/kg, \(P_3 = 1247.3\) kPa.

State 4 is at evaporator pressure after isenthalpic expansion, so \(h_4 = h_3 = 267.29\) kJ/kg. The quality at state 4 is
\[

\]

For the isentropic compressor exit (state 2s): \(P_{2s} = P_3 = 1247.3\) kPa and \(s_{2s} = s_1 = 1.7404\) kJ/(kg·K). Since \(s_{2s} > s_g\) at \(48^\circ\)C, state 2s is superheated. From the superheated R-134a tables (interpolation at the condenser pressure), \(h_{2s} = 432.0\) kJ/kg.

\[
\eta_s = 0.83 = \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{h_2 - h_1} \implies h_2 = h_1 + \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{0.83} = 389.17 + \frac{432.0 - 389.17}{0.83} = 440.77\text{ kJ/kg}.

\[

\]

The power and heat transfer rates are
\[
\dot{W}_\text{comp} = \dot{m} \cdot w_\text{comp} = 0.248 \times 51.6 = 12.8\text{ kW},
\]
\[
\dot{Q}_L = \dot{m} \cdot q_L = 0.248 \times 121.88 = 30.23\text{ kW}.
\]

Use the IIR reference state for R-134a properties: h = 200.0 kJ/kg and s = 1.0000 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at 0°C.

(a) h₁, h₂s, h₂, h₃, h₄

The vapor-compression refrigeration cycle has four main processes: isentropic compression in the ideal case (with given efficiency), isobaric heat rejection in the condenser, isenthalpic expansion in the throttle, and isobaric heat absorption in the evaporator.

The evaporator pressure is the saturation pressure at -22.0°C. State 1 is saturated vapor at this temperature. Using the IIR-reference R-134a saturation table (with linear interpolation between tabulated values at -24°C and -20°C):

h₁ = hg(-22°C) = 386.28 kJ/kg  
s₁ = sg(-22°C) = 1.74625 kJ/(kg·K)

The condenser pressure is the saturation pressure at 32.0°C. State 3 is saturated liquid at this temperature:

h₃ = hf(32°C) = 245.47 kJ/kg

State 4 is at the evaporator pressure with h₄ = h₃ (isenthalpic throttle), so h₄ = 245.47 kJ/kg.

For the ideal (isentropic) compressor exit (state 2s), s₂s = s₁ at the condenser pressure. This places state 2s in the superheated region. Using the superheated R-134a table at P ≈ 0.8 MPa (close to P_sat at 32°C) and interpolating for s = 0.95325 kJ/(kg·K) in the low-enthalpy reference before shifting by +150 kJ/kg for IIR reference:

h₂s = 428.55 kJ/kg

The compressor isentropic efficiency is given as η_c = 0.78:

\[
\eta_c = \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{h_2 - h_1}
\]

Solving for the actual exit enthalpy:

h₂ = h₁ + (h₂s - h₁)/0.78 = 440.47 kJ/kg

The per-unit-mass quantities are:

w_comp = h₂ - h₁ = 54.19 kJ/kg  
q_L = h₁ - h₄ = 140.81 kJ/kg  
q_H = h₂ - h₃ = 195.0 kJ/kg

The quality at state 4 (two-phase at evaporator pressure) is:

\[
x_4 = \frac{h_4 - h_f(-22^\circ\text{C})}{h_{fg}(-22^\circ\text{C})} = 0.338
\]

The refrigeration COP is:

COP_R = q_L / w_comp = 2.6

With \(\dot{m}\) = 0.08 kg/s, the power and heat transfer rates are:

Ẇ_comp = \(\dot{m}\) × w_comp = 4.335 kW  
Q̇_L = \(\dot{m}\) × q_L = 11.26 kW

The vapor-compression refrigeration cycle uses R-134a as the working fluid. Properties are obtained from saturation and superheated tables for R-134a consistent with the specified IIR reference state.

State 1 is saturated vapor at \(T_1 = -3^\circ\)C. From the saturation table:  
\(h_1 = h_g(-3^\circ\)C\() = 396.5\) kJ/kg and \(s_1 = s_g(-3^\circ\)C\() = 1.728\) kJ/(kg·K).

State 3 is saturated liquid at \(T_3 = 34^\circ\)C. From the saturation table:  
\(h_3 = h_f(34^\circ\)C\() = 247.2\) kJ/kg.

State 2s is found at \(P_{2s} = P_\text{sat}(34^\circ\)C\() \approx 0.864\) MPa and \(s_{2s} = s_1 = 1.728\) kJ/(kg·K). Since \(s_1 > s_g\) at the condenser pressure, state 2s is superheated. Interpolation in the superheated vapor table yields \(h_{2s} = 421.2\) kJ/kg.

The compressor isentropic efficiency is given as \(\eta_c = 0.86\):  
\[
\eta_c = \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{h_2 - h_1} \implies h_2 = h_1 + \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{0.86} = 396.5 + \frac{24.7}{0.86} = 425.2 \text{ kJ/kg}.
\]

The throttle process is isenthalpic, so \(h_4 = h_3 = 247.2\) kJ/kg.

The mass-specific compressor work is  
\(w_\text{comp} = h_2 - h_1 = 28.7\) kJ/kg.

The mass-specific cooling effect (evaporator heat absorption) is  
\(q_L = h_1 - h_4 = 149.3\) kJ/kg.

The mass-specific heat rejection (condenser heat rejection) is  
\(q_H = h_2 - h_3 = 178.0\) kJ/kg.

\(h_4 = h_f(-3^\circ\)C\() + x_4 \cdot h_{fg}(-3^\circ\)C\()\),

The refrigeration COP is  
\(\text{COP}_R = q_L / w_\text{comp} = 149.3 / 28.7 = 5.20\).

The compressor power input is  
\(\dot{W}_\text{comp} = \dot{m} \cdot w_\text{comp} = 0.345 \times 28.7 = 9.91\) kW.

The cooling capacity is  
\(\dot{Q}_L = \dot{m} \cdot q_L = 0.345 \times 149.3 = 51.51\) kW.

**Reasoning**

Saturation properties are obtained from R-134a tables using the specified IIR reference state. State 1 is saturated vapor at T_evap = -14°C, requiring linear interpolation between table values at -16°C and -12°C to give h₁, s₁. State 3 is saturated liquid at T_cond = 38°C, requiring linear interpolation between table values at 36°C and 40°C to give h₃, s₃. The throttle is isenthalpic, so h₄ = h₃. Quality x₄ is calculated from the definition x₄ = (h₄ - h_f)/(-14°C)/h_fg(-14°C). The cooling effect is q_L = h₁ - h₄ and heat rejection is q_H = h₂ - h₃.

For the compressor, s_{2s} = s₁ at P_cond = P_sat(38°C) ≈ 0.964 MPa (interpolated). The corresponding h_{2s} is found by interpolation in the superheated vapor tables between P = 0.9 MPa and 1.0 MPa. The given compressor efficiency is then used to find actual h₂ from η_comp = (h_{2s} - h₁)/(h₂ - h₁). Compressor work is w_comp = h₂ - h₁. COP_R = q_L/w_comp. With given ṁ, Ẇ_comp = ṁ·w_comp and Q̇_L = ṁ·q_L.

Entropy at state 2 (superheated at P_cond, h₂) is found by interpolation in superheated tables, yielding s₂. Entropy at state 4 (two-phase at -14°C) is s₄ = s_f + x₄(s_g - s_f) at -14°C. Entropy generation per unit mass for each component is calculated from the entropy balance on each control volume:

- Compressor (adiabatic): s_gen,comp = s₂ - s₁
- Condenser (heat rejected to sink at 301.1 K): s_gen,cond = (s₃ - s₂) + q_H/301.1
- Throttle (isenthalpic): s_gen,throttle = s₄ - s₃
- Evaporator (heat absorbed from space at 267.0 K): s_gen,evap = (s₁ - s₄) - q_L/267.0

Total s_gen is the sum of the component values. The energy balance error is zero within roundoff since q_H = q_L + w_comp by definition of the cycle. All interpolations are linear in the tabulated variables and properties are consistent with the IIR reference state.

Use the IIR reference state for R-134a properties: h = 200.0 kJ/kg and s = 1.0000 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at 0°C.

Determine all state enthalpies, entropies, compressor work, cooling effect, heat rejection, x₄, COP_R, power and capacity. Also find entropy generation in each component (compressor, condenser, throttle valve, evaporator) and total entropy generation per unit mass.

Present your final answers in the following format:

raw_response:
The VCR cycle has four components with the given temperatures fixing the saturation pressures: \(P_\text{evap} = P_\text{sat}(-4^\circ\text{C})\) and \(P_\text{cond} = P_\text{sat}(35^\circ\text{C})\). State 1 is saturated vapor at \(T_1 = -4^\circ\)C, so \(h_1\) and \(s_1\) are read directly from the saturated R-134a table (IIR reference). State 3 is saturated liquid at \(T_3 = 35^\circ\)C, so \(h_3\) and \(s_3\) are read directly from the saturated table. The throttle is isenthalpic, so \(h_4 = h_3\). Quality \(x_4\) is then calculated from the definition at \(T_4 = -4^\circ\)C:

\[x_4 = \frac{h_4 - h_f(-4^\circ\text{C})}{h_g(-4^\circ\text{C}) - h_f(-4^\circ\text{C})}\]

and \(s_4\) is calculated from

\[s_4 = s_f(-4^\circ\text{C}) + x_4[s_g(-4^\circ\text{C}) - s_f(-4^\circ\text{C})].\]

For the compressor, first locate the isentropic exit (state 2s) in the superheated R-134a table at \(P_{2s} = P_\text{cond}\) and \(s_{2s} = s_1\) to obtain \(h_{2s}\). The given compressor efficiency is then solved for the actual exit enthalpy:

\[\eta_\text{comp} = \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{h_2 - h_1} \implies h_2 = h_1 + \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{\eta_\text{comp}}.\]

State 2 is fixed by \(P_2 = P_\text{cond}\) and \(h_2\), allowing \(s_2\) to be read/interpolated from the superheated table. The mass-specific quantities follow directly from the first law applied to each device (steady flow, neglecting ke/pe):

\[w_\text{comp} = h_2 - h_1,\]
\[q_L = h_1 - h_4,\]
\[q_H = h_2 - h_3,\]
\[\text{COP}_R = \frac{q_L}{w_\text{comp}}.\]

The rates are obtained by multiplying by the given \(\dot{m}\):

\[\dot{W}_\text{comp} = \dot{m} \cdot w_\text{comp},\]
\[\dot{Q}_L = \dot{m} \cdot q_L.\]

Entropy generation per unit mass in each component is obtained from the steady-flow entropy balance, using the given boundary temperatures for the heat transfers (\(T_0 = 303\) K for the condenser and \(T_c = 274.8\) K for the evaporator):

- Compressor (adiabatic): \(s_\text{gen,comp} = s_2 - s_1\)
- Condenser: \(s_\text{gen,cond} = s_3 - s_2 + q_H/T_0\)
- Throttle (adiabatic): \(s_\text{gen,throttle} = s_4 - s_3\)
- Evaporator: \(s_\text{gen,evap} = s_1 - s_4 - q_L/T_c\)

The total is the sum of the component values (or equivalently \(q_H/T_0 - q_L/T_c\)). The energy balance error is the deviation of \(q_H - (q_L + w_\text{comp})\) from zero (expected to be exactly zero within table-reading precision).

- Throttle (adiabatic): \(s_\text{gen,throttle} = s_4 - s_3\)
- Evaporator: \(s_\text{gen,evap} = s_1 - s_4 - q_L/T_c\)

The total is the sum of the component values (or equivalently \(q_H/T_0 - q_L/T_c\)). The energy balance error is the deviation of \(q_H - (q_L + w_\text{comp})\) from zero (expected to be exactly zero within table-reading precision).

The VCR cycle operates with saturated vapor leaving the evaporator at \(T_1 = T_\text{evap} = -11^\circ\)C and saturated liquid leaving the condenser at \(T_3 = T_\text{cond} = 48^\circ\)C. The throttle process is isenthalpic (\(h_4 = h_3\)). The compressor has an isentropic efficiency of 83%. All properties are obtained from R-134a saturation and superheat tables using the specified IIR reference state.

State 1 is saturated vapor:
- Read \(h_1\), \(s_1\), \(h_f\) (evaporator), and \(s_f\) (evaporator).

State 3 is saturated liquid:
- Read \(h_3\), \(s_3\), and saturation pressure \(P_3 = P_2\).

State 4: \(h_4 = h_3\). Quality \(x_4\) is found from the definition at evaporator pressure:
\[
x_4 = \frac{h_4 - h_{f,\text{evap}}}{h_{g,\text{evap}} - h_{f,\text{evap}}}
\]
Then \(s_4 = s_{f,\text{evap}} + x_4(s_{g,\text{evap}} - s_{f,\text{evap}})\).

For the compressor, first find the isentropic exit (state 2s) at \(P_{2s} = P_3\) and \(s_{2s} = s_1\). Since \(s_{2s} > s_g\) at the condenser pressure, state 2s is superheated; interpolate in the superheated table for \(h_{2s}\). The isentropic work is \(h_{2s} - h_1\). The actual work follows from the definition of compressor efficiency:
\[
\eta_\text{comp} = \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{h_2 - h_1} \implies h_2 = h_1 + \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{\eta_\text{comp}}
\]
Interpolate in the superheated table at \(P_2 = P_3\) and \(h_2\) to obtain \(s_2\).

Per-unit-mass energy quantities:
\[
w_\text{comp} = h_2 - h_1, \quad q_L = h_1 - h_4, \quad q_H = h_2 - h_3
\]
COP:
\[
\text{COP}_R = \frac{q_L}{w_\text{comp}}
\]
With \(\dot{m} = 0.21\) kg/s the power and capacity are:
\[
\dot{W}_\text{comp} = \dot{m} \cdot w_\text{comp}, \quad \dot{Q}_L = \dot{m} \cdot q_L
\]

Entropy generation per unit mass for each component (adiabatic compressor and throttle; heat transfers in condenser and evaporator occur at the given reservoir temperatures \(T_0 = 307.6\) K and \(T_L = 268.5\) K):
\[
s_\text{gen,comp} = s_2 - s_1
\]
\[
s_\text{gen,cond} = (s_3 - s_2) + \frac{q_H}{T_0}
\]
\[
s_\text{gen,throttle} = s_4 - s_3
\]
\[
s_\text{gen,evap} = (s_1 - s_4) - \frac{q_L}{T_L}
\]
Total:
\[
s_\text{gen,total} = s_\text{gen,comp} + s_\text{gen,cond} + s_\text{gen,throttle} + s_\text{gen,evap}
\]
The first-law balance \(w_\text{comp} + q_L - q_H\) closes to zero within table interpolation precision; the reported energy balance error is therefore zero.

Properties are obtained from R-134a tables using the specified IIR reference state. This is implemented by starting with standard table values (hf = 50.00 kJ/kg and sf = 0.2030 kJ/(kg·K) at 0°C saturated liquid) and applying constant shifts of +150 kJ/kg to all enthalpies and +0.797 kJ/(kg·K) to all entropies. Saturation properties at T = 34°C are obtained by linear interpolation between tabulated values at 30°C and 40°C. This yields P_cond ≈ 869 kPa, h₃ = 249.40 kJ/kg, and s₃ = 1.1690 kJ/(kg·K). At T = -10°C, h₁ = 392.31 kJ/kg and s₁ = 1.7446 kJ/(kg·K).

For the isentropic compressor exit (state 2s), s₂s = s₁ at P = 869 kPa. Superheated vapor properties are obtained from tables at P = 0.8 MPa and P = 1.0 MPa (with linear interpolation in pressure at fixed s₂s = 0.9476 in unshifted coordinates), yielding h₂s = 279.81 kJ/kg (429.81 kJ/kg shifted). The isentropic work is w_s = h₂s - h₁ = 37.5 kJ/kg. With η_comp = 0.79, the actual compressor work is w_comp = w_s/η_comp = 47.47 kJ/kg, so h₂ = h₁ + w_comp = 439.78 kJ/kg. The actual s₂ = 1.7737 kJ/(kg·K) is found by determining the state at h₂ and P_cond (interpolating in superheated tables at P = 0.8 MPa and P = 1.0 MPa).

The quality at state 4 is x₄ = (h₄ - h_f,-10°C)/h_fg,-10°C = 0.304, and s₄ = s_f + x₄ s_fg = 1.1903 kJ/(kg·K) at T = -10°C.

Use the IIR reference state for R-134a properties: h = 200.0 kJ/kg and s = 1.0000 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at 0°C.

Determine all state enthalpies, entropies, and flow exergies. Calculate compressor work, cooling effect, heat rejection, x₄, and COP_R. The VCR cycle operates with R-134a as the refrigerant between the evaporator temperature of -15.0°C and condenser temperature of 40.0°C. The compressor has an isentropic efficiency of 76%, the refrigerant mass flow rate is 0.325 kg/s, the evaporator exit is saturated vapor, the condenser exit is saturated liquid, and the expansion is isenthalpic. The IIR reference state is used for properties.

From the R-134a tables:
- State 1 (saturated vapor at -15°C): \(h_1 = 389.19\) kJ/kg, \(s_1 = 1.7405\) kJ/(kg·K).
- State 3 (saturated liquid at 40°C): \(h_3 = 256.54\) kJ/kg, \(s_3 = 1.1909\) kJ/(kg·K).
- \(P_1 = P_4 = 0.1648\) MPa, \(P_2 = P_3 = 1.0166\) MPa.

State 4 is determined from the isenthalpic process: \(h_4 = h_3 = 256.54\) kJ/kg. The quality at state 4 is
\[
x_4 = \frac{h_4 - h_f(-15^\circ\text{C})}{h_g(-15^\circ\text{C}) - h_f(-15^\circ\text{C})} = \frac{256.54 - 178.69}{210.5} = 0.370.
\]
The entropy at state 4 is
\[
s_4 = s_f(-15^\circ\text{C}) + x_4[s_g(-15^\circ\text{C}) - s_f(-15^\circ\text{C})] = 0.9220 + 0.370 \times 0.8185 = 1.2247 \text{ kJ/(kg·K)}.
\]

For the isentropic compressor exit (state 2s), \(s_{2s} = s_1 = 1.7405\) kJ/(kg·K) at \(P_2 = 1.0166\) MPa. Interpolation in the superheated table (accounting for \(s_g = 1.7123\) at 40°C) yields \(h_{2s} = 428.42\) kJ/kg. The actual compressor work follows from the given efficiency:
\[
\eta_\text{comp} = \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{h_2 - h_1} \implies h_2 - h_1 = \frac{428.42 - 389.19}{0.76} = 51.62 \text{ kJ/kg},
\]
so \(h_2 = 440.81\) kJ/kg. Interpolation in the superheated table at \(P_2\), \(h_2\) gives \(s_2 = 1.7783\) kJ/(kg·K).

Per-unit-mass energy quantities are
\[
w_\text{comp} = h_2 - h_1 = 51.62 \text{ kJ/kg},
\]
\[
q_L = h_1 - h_4 = 132.65 \text{ kJ/kg},
\]
\[
q_H = h_2 - h_3 = 184.27 \text{ kJ/kg}.
\]
The COP is
\[
\text{COP}_R = \frac{q_L}{w_\text{comp}} = \frac{132.65}{51.62} = 2.570.
\]
With \(\dot{m} = 0.325\) kg/s,
\[
\dot{W}_\text{comp} = \dot{m} \times w_\text{comp} = 16.78 \text{ kW}, \quad \dot{Q}_L = \dot{m} \times q_L = 43.11 \text{ kW}.
\]

Entropy generation rates (per unit mass) for each component are obtained from the steady-flow entropy balance, using \(T_H = 302.6\) K for the condenser heat rejection and \(T_L = 263.5\) K for the evaporator heat absorption:
\[
s_\text{gen,comp} = s_2 - s_1 = 0.0378 \text{ kJ/(kg·K)},
\]
\[
s_\text{gen,cond} = (s_3 - s_2) + \frac{q_H}{T_H} = 0.0216 \text{ kJ/(kg·K)},
\]
\[
s_\text{gen,throttle} = s_4 - s_3 = 0.0338 \text{ kJ/(kg·K)},
\]
\[
s_\text{gen,evap} = (s_1 - s_4) - \frac{q_L}{T_L} = 0.0122 \text{ kJ/(kg·K)}.
\]
The total is the sum: \(s_\text{gen,total} = 0.1054\) kJ/(kg·K). The energy balance closes exactly, so the error is zero.

Flow exergies (physical) at each state are
\[
\text{ef}_i = (h_i - h_0) - T_0(s_i - s_0),
\]
where \(T_0 = 298.15\) K and \(h_0\), \(s_0\) are evaluated at the dead state (\(T_0 = 25^\circ\)C, \(P_0 = 0.1\) MPa) from the superheated table, yielding ef\(_1\) = 13.03 kJ/kg, ef\(_2\) = 53.38 kJ/kg, ef\(_3\) = 44.24 kJ/kg, ef\(_4\) = 34.19 kJ/kg.

Exergy destructions (per unit mass) are \(x_\text{dest} = T_0 \times s_\text{gen}\) for each component:
\[
x_\text{dest,comp} = 11.27 \text{ kJ/kg}, \quad x_\text{dest,cond} = 6.44 \text{ kJ/kg},
\]
\[
x_\text{dest,throttle} = 10.08 \text{ kJ/kg}, \quad x_\text{dest,evap} = 3.64 \text{ kJ/kg}.
\]
The total is the sum: \(x_\text{dest,total} = 31.42\) kJ/kg. These values are confirmed to be consistent with component exergy balances.

The Carnot COP for the refrigeration cycle between the cooled-space and environment temperatures is
\[
\text{COP}_\text{Carnot} = \frac{T_L}{T_H - T_L} = \frac{263.5}{302.6 - 263.5} = 6.738.
\]
The second-law efficiency is
\[
\eta_\text{II} = \frac{\text{COP}_R}{\text{COP}_\text{Carnot}} = 0.381.
\]

State 1 is saturated vapor at -18°C. Obtain h₁ and s₁ directly from the saturation table (interpolated between -20°C and -16°C values). State 3 is saturated liquid at 35°C. Obtain h₃ and s₃ directly from the saturation table.

For the isentropic compressor exit (state 2s), set s_{2s} = s₁ at condenser pressure and obtain h_{2s} from the superheated vapor table (interpolated at P = 0.9 MPa). The actual compressor exit enthalpy follows from the given isentropic efficiency:

h₂ = h₁ + (h_{2s} - h₁)/η_comp

Obtain s₂ at the actual h₂ and condenser pressure from the superheated table.

The throttle is isenthalpic, so h₄ = h₃. At evaporator pressure, use h₄ to obtain quality x₄ = (h₄ - h_f)/(-18°C)/h_fg(-18°C) and s₄ from the saturation table.

Per-unit-mass energy quantities are:

w_comp = h₂ - h₁  
q_L = h₁ - h₄  
q_H = h₂ - h₃  
COP_R = q_L/w_comp

Scale by the given mass flow rate to obtain Ẇ_comp and Q̇_L.

Entropy generation per unit mass in each component is found from entropy balance (using T_H = 297.3 K for the condenser and T_L = 260.6 K for the evaporator):

s_gen,comp = s₂ - s₁ (adiabatic)  
s_gen,cond = (s₃ - s₂) + q_H/T_H  
s_gen,throttle = s₄ - s₃ (isenthalpic)  
s_gen,evap = (s₁ - s₄) - q_L/T_L  

Total s_gen is the sum of component values. The energy balance error is |(q_H - q_L - w_comp)/q_L| (must be zero within table rounding).

Obtain h₀ and s₀ for R-134a at the dead state (T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa) from the superheated table. Flow (physical) exergy at each state is:

ef_i = (h_i - h₀) - T₀(s_i - s₀)

Exergy destruction per unit mass in each component is x_dest = T₀·s_gen (component). Total x_dest is the sum of component values.

The Carnot COP for refrigeration between the cooled-space and environment temperatures is:

COP_Carnot = T_L/(T_H - T_L)

Second-law efficiency is η_II = COP_R/COP_Carnot.

Use the IIR reference state for R-134a properties: h = 200.0 kJ/kg and s = 1.0000 kJ/(kg·K) for saturated liquid at 0°C.

Determine all state enthalpies, entropies, and flow exergies. Calculate compressor work, cooling effect, heat rejection, x₄, and COP_R. raw_response:
The VCR cycle operates with saturated vapor at the evaporator exit (state 1 at \(T_1 = -22^\circ\)C) and saturated liquid at the condenser exit (state 3 at \(T_3 = 41^\circ\)C). The throttle is isenthalpic (\(h_4 = h_3\)). Compressor isentropic efficiency is given as \(\eta_\text{comp} = 0.83\).

Saturation properties are obtained from R-134a tables (IIR reference) with linear interpolation required at \(T = -22^\circ\)C (between table values at -24°C and -20°C) and at \(T = 41^\circ\)C (between table values at 40°C and 44°C). This yields:
- At state 1: \(h_1 = hg(-22^\circ\)C), \(s_1 = sg(-22^\circ\)C), \(P_1 = 0.123\) MPa.
- At state 3: \(h_3 = hf(41^\circ\)C), \(s_3 = sf(41^\circ\)C), \(P_3 = 1.044\) MPa.

State 4 is in the two-phase region at \(P_4 = P_1\). Quality \(x_4\) follows from the definition \(x_4 = (h_4 - h_f)/h_{fg}\) at \(-22^\circ\)C. Entropy \(s_4\) is then \(s_4 = s_f + x_4(s_g - s_f)\) at \(-22^\circ\)C.

For the isentropic compressor exit (state 2s), \(s_{2s} = s_1\) at \(P_{2s} = P_3\). Since \(s_{2s} > s_g\) at 41°C, state 2s is superheated. Interpolation in the superheated R-134a tables at \(P \approx 1.04\) MPa gives \(h_{2s}\). The actual enthalpy follows from the efficiency definition:
\[
\eta_\text{comp} = \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{h_2 - h_1} \implies h_2 = h_1 + \frac{h_{2s} - h_1}{0.83}.
\]
State 2 is superheated at \(P_2 = P_3\), \(h_2\); interpolation in superheated tables gives \(s_2\).

Per-unit-mass energy transfers are:
\[
w_\text{comp} = h_2 - h_1, \quad q_L = h_1 - h_4, \quad q_H = h_2 - h_3.
\]
COP follows as \(\text{COP}_R = q_L/w_\text{comp}\). With given \(\dot{m}\), the rates are \(\dot{W}_\text{comp} = \dot{m} \cdot w_\text{comp}\) and \(\dot{Q}_L = \dot{m} \cdot q_L\).

Entropy generation per unit mass in each component (steady-state entropy balance) uses the appropriate boundary temperatures (\(T_\text{env} = 308.1\) K for condenser heat rejection, \(T_L = 258.3\) K for evaporator heat absorption; compressor and throttle are adiabatic):
- Compressor: \(s_\text{gen,comp} = s_2 - s_1\).
- Condenser: \(s_\text{gen,cond} = (s_3 - s_2) + q_H/T_\text{env}\).
- Throttle: \(s_\text{gen,throttle} = s_4 - s_3\).
- Evaporator: \(s_\text{gen,evap} = (s_1 - s_4) - q_L/T_L\).

Total \(s_\text{gen}\) is the sum. The energy balance error is \(|q_H - (q_L + w_\text{comp})|\) (equals zero within round-off).

Dead-state properties (\(h_0\), \(s_0\)) are obtained by interpolation in the superheated table at \(T_0 = 25^\circ\)C, \(P_0 = 0.1\) MPa. Flow exergy at each state is:
\[
ef_i = (h_i - h_0) - T_0(s_i - s_0).
\]
Exergy destruction per unit mass in each component is \(x_\text{dest} = T_0 \cdot s_\text{gen}\) (verified by direct exergy balance on each CV, which matches within round-off). Total \(x_\text{dest}\) is the sum.

Carnot COP (between cooled-space and environment temperatures) is:
\[
\text{COP}_\text{Carnot} = \frac{T_L}{T_\text{env} - T_L}.
\]
Second-law efficiency is \(\eta_{II} = \text{COP}_R / \text{COP}_\text{Carnot}\).

**Reasoning:**  
Saturation properties are obtained from standard R-134a tables consistent with the specified IIR reference state (hf = 200 kJ/kg, sf = 1 kJ/kg·K for saturated liquid at 0°C). State 1 is saturated vapor at Te = −25°C, fixing h₁, s₁, P₁. State 3 is saturated liquid at Tc = 40°C, fixing h₃, s₃, P₂ = P₃. The throttle is isenthalpic, so h₄ = h₃. Quality x₄ follows directly from the definition x₄ = (h₄ − hf at Te)/(hg − hf at Te).  

For the compressor, s₂ₛ = s₁ at P₂ = Pcond. The corresponding h₂ₛ is read from the superheated table (or obtained by interpolation) at Pcond and s = s₁. The given isentropic efficiency η_comp = (h₂ₛ − h₁)/(h₂ − h₁) is solved for the actual exit enthalpy h₂. Compressor work, cooling effect, and heat rejection per unit mass are then w_comp = h₂ − h₁, q_L = h₁ − h₄, q_H = h₂ − h₃. Mass-flow rates give the power and capacity: Ẇ_comp = ṁw_comp, Q̇_L = ṁq_L. COP_R = q_L/w_comp.  

Entropy at state 2 is obtained from the superheated table at P₂ and h₂ (or by interpolation); s₄ follows from the two-phase entropy relation at Te using the already-determined x₄. Entropy generation per unit mass for each component follows from the steady-flow entropy balance written on a control-volume basis for that device, using the reservoir temperatures TL = 255.9 K for the evaporator and TH = 302.5 K for the condenser:  
- Compressor (adiabatic): s_gen,comp = s₂ − s₁  
- Condenser: s_gen,cond = s₃ − s₂ + q_H/TH  
- Throttle (adiabatic): s_gen,throttle = s₄ − s₃  
- Evaporator: s_gen,evap = s₁ − s₄ − q_L/TL  
The sum is the total entropy generation per unit mass. The energy-balance error is identically zero (within rounding) because the first-law relations close exactly.  

Dead-state properties (h₀, s₀) are evaluated at T₀ = 25°C, P₀ = 0.1 MPa in the superheated-vapor region from the same tables. Flow exergy at each state is ef = (h − h₀) − T₀(s − s₀). Exergy destruction per unit mass in each component is x_dest = T₀ × s_gen for that component (verified by direct exergy balances on each device). The Carnot COP is evaluated between the external reservoirs: COP_Carnot = TL/(TH − TL). The second-law efficiency is η_II = COP_R/COP_Carnot. All numerical values are obtained by substitution of the tabulated properties into the foregoing relations.
